results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must inform controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors
results.condition_check: compatible: 'in the case of general written authorisation' matches the regulatory trigger
results.modality_check: equal force: 'shall' matches 'shall'

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the processor shall process personal data, matching the regulation's processor processing personal data; recipient is null on both sides.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.

results.other_constraints_check: The contract's 'only on documented instructions from the Controller' matches and is at least as strict as 'on documented instructions from the controller'.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

This is a different obligation (cooperation with authority vs assisting controller to consult authority), not the same real-world duty.
results.condition_check: Regulation applies where processing would result in high risk absent controller mitigation measures; contract applies on the Controller’s request. Conditions are not compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); contract is a plain statement but even if treated as obligatory, the substantive duty is different and not the required assistance duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs: regulation requires assistance prior to processing; contract has no prior-to-processing timing and instead is request-based. Other aspects do not bridge the gap.
results.contract_source_text: to cooperate (including representatives of Processor), on the Controller’s request, with the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align on processor engaging sub-processors, but the contract is only a permission and is conditioned on a consultancy agreement rather than controller written authorization
results.condition_check: different condition: consultancy agreement under the main agreement vs prior specific or general written authorization of the controller
results.modality_check: weaker: 'may' permits engagement, while the regulation prohibits engagement absent authorization
results.other_constraints_check: no matching prior written authorization requirement; manner/target do not cure the mismatch
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller and the Processor has entered into a Consultancy Agreement as part of the Main Agreement, the Processor may engage Sub- processors for the provision of the consultancy Services to the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: yes — both require the processor to impose data protection obligations on engaged sub-processors; the contract uses the agreement's own obligations rather than expressly Article 28(3), but this is co-extensive and substantively the same duty
results.condition_check: compatible — both are triggered when sub-processors are engaged for processing activities on behalf of the controller
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: compatible — contract requires a contract or other legal act under EU or Member State law and adds sufficient guarantees/technical and organisational measures, which is at least as strict as the regulation
results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall process personal data; same obligation as regulation
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract adds an exception for Union or Member State law, which is a standard permitted qualifier and does not weaken the core duty
results.modality_check: Equal force: shall
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' matches 'on documented instructions from the controller' and is at least as strict
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: yes — both impose on the processor a duty to implement/take appropriate technical and organisational measures to secure processing and meet Article 32/GDPR security requirements
results.condition_check: no condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: regulation uses shall; contract uses agrees to, which is an обязательство/commitment of equivalent force in this context
results.other_constraints_check: contract manner is at least as strict: measures must ensure processing meets GDPR requirements and protect data subjects' rights, which aligns with or exceeds the security obligation
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: same obligation to make compliance information available; 'records' are a sufficient concrete vehicle for information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations, and availability to the controller (plus supervisory authority) matches or exceeds the recipient scope
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'on request' is a reasonable triggering condition for making information available and does not weaken the duty
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: no conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; broader recipient scope is stricter, not looser
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall make the records available to the Controller and the supervisory authority on request. 8. Costs for assistance and audits

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require including/implementing measures or ability to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services; the contract is the processor/controller-side security obligation and matches the regulation's security objective.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional in substance; the parent clause's risk-based framing is the standard GDPR security qualifier and does not weaken the duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' is equal-or-stronger than 'can' in the regulation context because the contract imposes the obligation to include the ability to ensure the listed security properties.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'as appropriate' aligns with the regulation's open-ended security standard and is not looser.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the nature of the breach; the contract parent/sub-item requires that the notification describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including where possible, and the sub-item adds likely consequences. This is the same content obligation, with the contract at least as specific.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses shall; contract sub-item is within a mandatory parent clause and is a descriptive statement, which counts as mandatory and not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints; the contract is at least as specific and does not narrow the obligation.
results.contract_source_text: the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations to the controller. The contract instead says the processor may fulfil a documentation request by referring the controller to audit reports or similar documentation from sub-processors showing the sub-processors comply with GDPR. This is related to compliance evidence, but it is narrower and different in scope: it concerns only sub-processor compliance documentation and a referral mechanism, not making available the full information necessary to demonstrate the processor's Article 28 compliance.
results.condition_check: Compatible in part: both are about compliance documentation, but the contract is triggered only if the controller requests documentation of sub-processor compliance, which is narrower than the regulation's general duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' but the operative phrase is 'be entitled to fulfil', which is permissive/optional rather than an unconditional obligation to make available the required information.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing or location issues. The manner is materially narrower because it allows fulfilment by referral to sub-processor audit reports or similar documentation, rather than making available the information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. Change of instructions

results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' on the contract matches the regulatory 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only on documented instructions from the Controller' is at least as strict as 'only on instructions from the controller'; the parent clause also states the agreement constitutes the instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.condition_check: compatible
results.modality_check: must is at least as strong as shall
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause differences

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires technical/organizational measures to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services. The contract parent clause requires appropriate security measures, and the sub-item specifically requires the ability to restore availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. That is a concrete security/resilience measure within the same obligation set, and the parent security duty covers the broader confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience requirement.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation has no special triggering condition; the contract sub-item applies in the event of a physical or technical incident, which is a reasonable and narrower implementation of availability/resilience measures rather than a weaker condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract sub-item is a plain mandatory sub-item within a parent clause that requires implementation of appropriate technical and organisational measures, so it carries mandatory force at least equal to the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'In a timely manner' is consistent with, and not looser than, the regulation's availability/resilience expectation. The incident trigger is a specific operational context that fits within the broader security obligation.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: both concern the processor's liability for damage/loss arising from processing, but the contract shifts liability to the controller's failure rather than the processor's own GDPR noncompliance or acting outside instructions

results.modality_check: contract is restrictive/negative ('shall not' be liable) but in a different liability allocation and not equal-or-greater force for the regulated duty
results.other_constraints_check: object and cause are materially different: regulation covers damage caused by processing under specified processor fault conditions; contract covers loss or damages caused by the controller's failure to comply
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable for loss or damages if caused by the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations according to applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulations or this Agreement. Nor shall the Processor be liable for loss or damages in the event of the Controller’s breach of the Main Agreement and/or other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data, or the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations towards a supervisory authority, or for penalties imposed by a supervisory authority due to the Controller’s breach or failure to comply.

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern processor notification/informing the controller about intended changes to addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization.

results.modality_check: Compatible: contract uses 'shall' and is not permissive.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing, and the contract parent/sub-item requires implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including testing, assessing and evaluating their effectiveness. results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract sub-item is within a mandatory parent clause and is therefore obligatory, not merely permissive.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring the security of processing. The contract parent clause imposes technical and organisational security measures appropriate to risk, and the sub-item specifies restoring availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. This is a security measure, but it does not itself impose processor assistance to the controller; it is the substance of security measures rather than assistance. However, because the parent clause is jointly on controller and processor and the sub-item is a concrete security measure, there is substantive overlap with ensuring processing security.
results.condition_check: Both are triggered generally in the context of processing security; the contract sub-item applies in the event of a physical or technical incident, which is a compatible and narrower security-related condition.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall' via the security obligation), and the sub-item is part of that mandatory obligation. The plain statement sub-item counts as mandatory in this context.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's 'in a timely manner' is compatible with the regulation's no-specific-timing assistance duty and is not weaker. The physical or technical incident trigger is consistent with security of processing.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.
